$comment: GENERATED by scripts/gen_docs.py from pearlarr/config.py; do not edit; regenerate: uv run python scripts/gen_docs.py
title: Pearlarr configuration

AdvancedSettings.description: Rate limiting, caching, run caps, and logging.

sleep_time.description: Seconds slept between API queries, as rate limiting. `0` disables the sleep.
sleep_time.title: Sleep Time

cache_time.description: Days downloaded mapping sources are kept before re-downloading. `0` re-downloads every run.
cache_time.title: Cache Time

interactive.description: Prompt for a choice when several torrents match, instead of taking the best automatically.
interactive.title: Interactive

max_torrents_to_add.description: Cap on torrents added per run; `0` removes the cap. Keeps a first run on a large library from flooding qBittorrent; later runs pick up where the cap stopped. Preview runs ignore the cap, so a preview always reports the whole library.
max_torrents_to_add.title: Max Torrents To Add

detect_arr_activity.description: Re-check titles whose files Sonarr or Radarr changed since the last run. Polls each arr's history at run start. Turn off if a release you replaced arr-side keeps being re-grabbed.
detect_arr_activity.title: Detect Arr Activity

log_level.description: Minimum level written to the console and the file log, case-insensitive.
log_level.title: Log Level

log_format.description: Console output format. `auto` resolves to `rich` on a terminal and `plain` when piped or under Docker; `plain` and `json` also disable the live progress views.

log_retention_days.description: How many days of dated log backups to keep. Each run rotates the previous run's log to a dated backup; backups older than this many days are deleted once the run's configuration loads.
log_retention_days.title: Log Retention Days

AdvancedSettings.title: AdvancedSettings

ArrSettings.description: Radarr connection and behavior. `url` and `api_key` are required only when a Radarr run actually executes; a Sonarr-only config still loads.

url.description: Base URL of the instance's web UI, e.g. `http://localhost:8989` for Sonarr or `http://localhost:7878` for Radarr. Blank leaves this arr unconfigured and its runs are skipped.
url.title: Url

api_key.description: API key of the instance, from Settings > General in its web UI. Blank leaves this arr unconfigured and its runs are skipped.
api_key.title: Api Key

verify_ssl.description: Verify the instance's HTTPS certificate. Turn off only for a self-signed instance whose CA cannot be trusted via the OS store or `SSL_CERT_FILE`.
verify_ssl.title: Verify Ssl

ignore_unmonitored.description: Skip items the arr does not monitor. For Radarr this means unmonitored movies; for Sonarr, unmonitored series or entries whose episodes are all unmonitored.
ignore_unmonitored.title: Ignore Unmonitored

torrent_category.description: qBittorrent category applied to torrents grabbed for this arr. Blank applies no category.
torrent_category.title: Torrent Category

ArrSettings.title: ArrSettings

ImportWaitMode.description: When (if ever) the manual-import wait/import runs, resolved cli > config.
ImportWaitMode.title: ImportWaitMode

ImportsSettings.description: Waiting for grabbed torrents and Sonarr manual import. Sonarr only: a Radarr run ignores this group.

wait_mode.description: When, if ever, to wait for grabbed torrents to finish downloading and then drive Sonarr's import.

wait_timeout.description: Seconds to wait per torrent for qBittorrent to finish downloading it.
wait_timeout.title: Wait Timeout

ready_timeout.description: Seconds to then wait for Sonarr to rescan and import the finished files.
ready_timeout.title: Ready Timeout

poll_interval.description: Seconds between polls of qBittorrent and the Sonarr queue while waiting.
poll_interval.title: Poll Interval

progress_poll_interval.description: Seconds between lightweight wait-screen refreshes (progress bars, speed, ETA) within each poll interval. `0` disables the extra refreshes; rows then advance once per `poll_interval` while spinners and timers still animate. A value at or above `poll_interval` behaves as disabled.
progress_poll_interval.title: Progress Poll Interval

mode.description: How the import takes finished files into the library. `auto` lets Sonarr choose between moving and copying (a still-seeding torrent is copied); `move` and `copy` force it. Forwarded to Sonarr verbatim.
mode.title: Mode

post_import_category.description: qBittorrent category applied to a torrent once its import is verified complete. Useful to give finished torrents different seeding rules. Blank keeps the add-time category. Only applies when `wait_mode` is not `off`.
post_import_category.title: Post Import Category

default_quality.description: Sonarr quality name, e.g. `Bluray-2160p`, filling the gaps when a file's quality cannot be detected. Useful on a 4K instance. Blank adds no default; a name matching no Sonarr quality definition is warned about and ignored.
default_quality.title: Default Quality

languages_dual.description: Languages tagged on files imported from dual-audio releases. An empty list falls back to the default; a file is never imported with no language (Sonarr would read it as Unknown and could re-grab it).
languages_dual.title: Languages Dual

languages_single.description: Languages tagged on files imported from single-audio releases. Empty falls back like `languages_dual`.
languages_single.title: Languages Single

pending_max_age_days.description: Days a pending import may wait for its download before its record is dropped.
pending_max_age_days.title: Pending Max Age Days

digest_interval.description: Target seconds between wait-progress digest lines on a non-interactive console (Docker, cron).
digest_interval.title: Digest Interval

ImportsSettings.title: ImportsSettings

MappingsSettings.description: ID and episode mapping sources. `anibridge_mappings` is the primary; the others fill anything it misses.

anime_mappings.description: Kometa `Anime-IDs` source, a fallback for IDs the primary graph misses. Blank auto-downloads it; `false` disables it; an inline mapping is accepted.
anime_mappings.title: Anime Mappings

anidb_mappings.description: AniDB `anime-lists` XML source, a fallback used for specials. Blank auto-downloads it; `false` disables it.
anidb_mappings.title: Anidb Mappings

anibridge_mappings.description: `anibridge-mappings` v3 graph, the primary source of IDs and per-season episode maps. Blank auto-downloads it; `false` disables it; an inline mapping is accepted.
anibridge_mappings.title: Anibridge Mappings

MappingsSettings.title: MappingsSettings

NotificationsSettings.description: Notification webhooks. Either, both, or neither may be set.

discord_url.description: Discord webhook URL grab notifications are posted to. Blank disables them.
discord_url.title: Discord Url

wait_webhook_url.description: Webhook URL (ntfy, gotify, Home Assistant, ...) receiving the wait-pass summary. Blank disables it.
wait_webhook_url.title: Wait Webhook Url

wait_notify.description: Send a notification when a wait pass completes. Absent, it turns on automatically whenever any webhook URL is set; an explicit value wins.
wait_notify.title: Wait Notify

NotificationsSettings.title: NotificationsSettings

QbittorrentSettings.description: qBittorrent connection. All of `host`, `username`, and `password` are needed to grab; leave any blank to run in preview mode (everything is reported, nothing is added).

host.description: WebUI address of qBittorrent, e.g. `http://localhost:8080`. Leave any of `host`, `username`, or `password` blank to run in preview mode: runs report what they would grab, but nothing is added.
host.title: Host

username.description: WebUI username. Blank switches to preview mode (see `host`).
username.title: Username

password.description: WebUI password. Blank switches to preview mode (see `host`).
password.title: Password

tags.description: Tags applied to every torrent added. Blank applies no tags.
tags.title: Tags

options.description: Extra keyword arguments passed to the qbittorrent-api client. For example `VERIFY_WEBUI_CERTIFICATE: false` for a WebUI behind a self-signed HTTPS certificate.
options.title: Options

QbittorrentSettings.title: QbittorrentSettings

private_releases.description: What to do when a title's preferred release sits only on private trackers. Private releases are never grabbed: SeaDex carries no download link for them, and no private-tracker auth is supported.

prefer_dual_audio.description: Prefer dual-audio releases; when off, prefer Japanese-audio releases.
prefer_dual_audio.title: Prefer Dual Audio

want_best.description: Prefer releases SeaDex marks as best.
want_best.title: Want Best

ignore_tags.description: SeaDex release tags to skip, e.g. `Dolby Vision` or `Deband Required`. Empty skips none.
ignore_tags.title: Ignore Tags

trackers.description: Tracker names considered during release selection, case-insensitive. Empty or absent considers every supported tracker. A listed tracker is not necessarily grabbable: private releases are never downloaded, and grabs come only from the public trackers Pearlarr can parse.
trackers.title: Trackers

ignore_anilist_ids.description: AniList IDs never processed. Empty ignores none.
ignore_anilist_ids.title: Ignore Anilist Ids

ignore_seadex_update_times.description: Re-check entries even when SeaDex has not updated them since the last check.
ignore_seadex_update_times.title: Ignore Seadex Update Times

use_torrent_hash_to_filter.description: Match what is already downloaded by torrent hash instead of by release group name.
use_torrent_hash_to_filter.title: Use Torrent Hash To Filter

SonarrSettings.description: Sonarr connection and behavior. `url` and `api_key` are required only when a Sonarr run actually executes; a Radarr-only config still loads.

url.description: Base URL of the instance's web UI, e.g. `http://localhost:8989` for Sonarr or `http://localhost:7878` for Radarr. Blank leaves this arr unconfigured and its runs are skipped.
url.title: Url

api_key.description: API key of the instance, from Settings > General in its web UI. Blank leaves this arr unconfigured and its runs are skipped.
api_key.title: Api Key

verify_ssl.description: Verify the instance's HTTPS certificate. Turn off only for a self-signed instance whose CA cannot be trusted via the OS store or `SSL_CERT_FILE`.
verify_ssl.title: Verify Ssl

ignore_unmonitored.description: Skip items the arr does not monitor. For Radarr this means unmonitored movies; for Sonarr, unmonitored series or entries whose episodes are all unmonitored.
ignore_unmonitored.title: Ignore Unmonitored

torrent_category.description: qBittorrent category applied to torrents grabbed for this arr. Blank applies no category.
torrent_category.title: Torrent Category

ignore_movies_in_radarr.description: Skip movies that already exist in Radarr instead of also grabbing them on the Sonarr side.
ignore_movies_in_radarr.title: Ignore Movies In Radarr

SonarrSettings.title: SonarrSettings

description: The full validated config: one submodel per settings group.

config_version.description: Schema version of the config file's keys and values. Stamped by the starter template and `pearlarr config migrate`; a file from an older Pearlarr is migrated automatically in memory at every load.
config_version.title: Config Version

sonarr.description: Sonarr connection and behavior. `url` and `api_key` are required only when a Sonarr run actually executes; a Radarr-only config still loads.

radarr.description: Radarr connection and behavior. `url` and `api_key` are required only when a Radarr run actually executes; a Sonarr-only config still loads.

qbittorrent.description: qBittorrent connection. All of `host`, `username`, and `password` are needed to grab; leave any blank to run in preview mode (everything is reported, nothing is added).

imports.description: Waiting for grabbed torrents and Sonarr manual import. Sonarr only: a Radarr run ignores this group.

notifications.description: Notification webhooks. Either, both, or neither may be set.

advanced.description: Rate limiting, caching, run caps, and logging.

mappings.description: ID and episode mapping sources. `anibridge_mappings` is the primary; the others fill anything it misses.
